Title: Metabolic Engineering of Natural Product Biosynthesis in Microorganisms
Journal: Annual Review of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables functional activation of heterologously expressed PKS/NRPS systems and thereby supports production of natural products such as erythromycin intermediates and echinomycin in E. coli
Common_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Posttranslational activation of carrier-protein domains in biosynthetic assembly lines
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli with metabolite production
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. sfp was inserted into E. coli to activate PKS/NRPS carrier proteins. 2. It was required for heterologous production of 6-deoxyerythronolide B and echinomycin-related systems. 3. The paper presents it as a key enabling gene rather than a pathway catalyst for the final metabolite scaffold.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper; gene discussed as a representative engineering component across multiple studies.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Competes for FPP; knockout increases amorphadiene synthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout with product titer measurement
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. erg9 encodes endogenous squalene synthase that consumes FPP. 2. Deletion reduced competing flux. 3. This increased amorphadiene production in engineered yeast.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core engineering target in review examples of competing-pathway deletion.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydrolyzes synthetic thioester substrate during simvastatin bioconversion; knockout improves simvastatin production
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BioH unexpectedly hydrolyzed the synthetic thioester substrate used for simvastatin production. 2. Identification and deletion of BioH improved product formation. 3. The gene is a host-side interference factor relevant to pathway engineering.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed in context of deleting host functions that reduce target production.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes bioconversion of monacolin J intermediate toward simvastatin in engineered E. coli
Common_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Late-stage acyl transfer in semisynthetic statin bioconversion
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression and in vivo bioconversion assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LovD was expressed heterologously in E. coli to convert monacolin J toward simvastatin. 2. Product formation was improved after removal of competing BioH hydrolase activity. 3. The review highlights LovD as a useful engineering enzyme for semisynthetic drug manufacture.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as common here because the review treats it mainly as a bioconversion engineering component rather than a focal native pathway-gene analysis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Convert propionyl-CoA to (2S)-methylmalonyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides methylmalonyl-CoA extender units needed for heterologous 6-dEB production in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pccA and pccB were transferred from S. coelicolor to E. coli. 2. They generated the nonnative extender unit (2S)-methylmalonyl-CoA. 3. This enabled heterologous biosynthesis of the erythromycin aglycone 6-dEB.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper example of precursor engineering in heterologous PK production.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Assemble the erythromycin macrolide core 6-deoxyerythronolide B from propionyl/methylmalonyl extender units through modular polyketide synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct production of the erythromycin core in heterologous E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DEBS1-3 comprise the modular PKS for erythromycin core biosynthesis. 2. Their heterologous expression in E. coli enabled production of 6-dEB. 3. Addition of downstream tailoring genes later enabled erythromycin C production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review also discusses engineering of DEBS domains for structural diversification.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Provides acyl carrier protein function used to support echinomycin total biosynthesis system
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports total biosynthesis of echinomycin in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fabC was included in an E. coli system for total echinomycin biosynthesis. 2. It functioned as a supporting biosynthetic component. 3. The review cites it as part of successful reconstitution of the antitumor NRP pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The text gives limited mechanistic detail.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Streptomyces coelicolor; Escherichia coli; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Aspergillus nidulans
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Streptomyces coelicolor; Escherichia coli; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Aspergillus nidulans
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Multifunctional polyketide synthase producing 6-methylsalicylic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Heterologous expression enabled production of 6-methylsalicylic acid in multiple hosts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in multiple hosts with product quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 6-MSAS was the first fungal synthase to produce a metabolite in heterologous hosts. 2. Expression in bacteria, yeast, and fungi yielded 6-methylsalicylic acid. 3. Yeast and A. nidulans gave particularly strong production without extensive optimization.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Introns were removed for expression in nonfungal hosts.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts phenylalanine to cinnamic acid for entry into flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributed to construction of a hybrid pathway producing naringenin in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Artificial pathway assembly and product detection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A yeast PAL was used to initiate an artificial naringenin pathway in E. coli. 2. It provided cinnamate precursor from phenylalanine. 3. Together with downstream heterologous genes, it enabled naringenin production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper; exact gene symbol not specified in excerpt.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates cinnamate or coumarate to the corresponding CoA thioester for flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supported hybrid naringenin biosynthesis in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Artificial pathway assembly and product detection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The ligase activated phenylpropanoid acids for chalcone formation. 2. It was combined with PAL, CHS, and CHI from different organisms. 3. The assembled pathway produced naringenin in E. coli.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper; exact gene symbol not provided.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA units to form chalcone scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled chalcone formation in hybrid E. coli pathway producing naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Artificial pathway assembly and product detection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS from licorice was used in the heterologous naringenin pathway. 2. It generated the chalcone intermediate from activated phenylpropanoids and malonyl-CoA. 3. This was essential for naringenin production in E. coli.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper; exact gene symbol not stated.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes chalcone to flavanone naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completed heterologous naringenin production in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Artificial pathway assembly and product detection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHI from Pueraria lobata completed the final conversion to naringenin. 2. It was part of a four-enzyme artificial pathway in E. coli. 3. The reconstructed pathway produced 57 mg/L naringenin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper example of combinatorial pathway assembly.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Carboxylates acetyl-CoA to malonyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased malonyl-CoA and enhanced production of actinorhodin and flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACCase generates malonyl-CoA, a central building block for polyketides. 2. Overexpression in S. coelicolor increased actinorhodin about sixfold. 3. Related engineering in E. coli substantially increased intracellular malonyl-CoA and flavonoid production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as a general precursor-supply target across studies.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first step of the MEP pathway to form 1-deoxy-D-xylulose-5-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point supported by overexpression studies
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased isoprenoid production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. dxs is a major engineering target in the endogenous E. coli MEP pathway. 2. Overexpression increased isoprenoid output. 3. The review presents it as part of successful precursor engineering for terpenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Text gives gene symbol as dxs.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 1-deoxy-D-xylulose-5-phosphate to MEP pathway intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased isoprenoid production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The review cites overexpression of dxr/dvr along with dxs and idi as improving isoprenoid production. 2. It functions in the MEP precursor pathway. 3. Increased precursor flux supported higher terpenoid output.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The excerpt prints the symbol as dvr, but the enzyme name corresponds to dxr.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Interconverts IPP and DMAPP in isoprenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased isoprenoid production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. idi balances IPP and DMAPP pools in engineered isoprenoid pathways. 2. Overexpression improved isoprenoid production in E. coli. 3. It is highlighted as a useful precursor-pathway engineering gene.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: General review example.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces HMG-CoA to mevalonate in the MVA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Engineered form increased amorphadiene production about fivefold in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chromosomal integration of engineered enzyme with product quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HMG-CoA reductase is identified as a bottleneck in the yeast MVA pathway. 2. Chromosomal integration of an engineered form increased amorphadiene production fivefold. 3. It is a major target for terpenoid precursor engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review does not specify exact allele name in excerpt.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the first step of the shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key precursor-supply step supported by overexpression response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased balhimycin-specific productivity by 250%
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. This first shikimate-pathway enzyme was overexpressed to boost aromatic precursor supply. 2. Balhimycin productivity increased by 250%. 3. The example illustrates successful precursor engineering for amino-acid-derived natural products.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact gene symbol not provided in excerpt.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Assembles the tripeptide precursor for penicillin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as bottleneck controlling penicillin production in A. nidulans
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACVS was identified as the bottleneck enzyme for penicillin biosynthesis in A. nidulans. 2. This contrasts with P. chrysogenum, where a downstream acyltransferase is limiting. 3. The example highlights species-specific pathway control points.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review emphasizes host-specific bottlenecks.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes downstream acyltransfer step in penicillin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: penDE-encoded acyltransferase was shown to be rate-limiting for penicillin production in P. chrysogenum
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In P. chrysogenum, the penDE-encoded acyltransferase rather than ACVS is rate-limiting. 2. This demonstrates species-specific control points in the same biosynthetic pathway. 3. It is highlighted as a target for pathway optimization.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contrasted explicitly with A. nidulans.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a late tailoring step in doxorubicin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Product inhibition and toxicity limited DoxA performance; overexpression of resistance genes enabled further yield improvement
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Engineering of resistance genes with yield analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DoxA was identified as a late-stage enzyme affected by product inhibition during doxorubicin biosynthesis. 2. Enhancing resistance alleviated this bottleneck. 3. The example shows that pathway productivity can be constrained by end-product toxicity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistic enzyme reaction not detailed in excerpt.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Installs epoxide functionality in pimaricin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disruption generated macrolide analog lacking the epoxide group
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene disruption and analog isolation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PimD is a tailoring P450 epoxidase in the pimaricin cluster. 2. Gene disruption yielded 4,5-deepoxypimaricin. 3. The example illustrates structural diversification by downstream tailoring-gene inactivation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a representative mutasynthesis/gene disruption example.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Provides chlorinated building block for salinosporamide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disruption of salL coupled with 5'-FDA feeding enabled production of fluorosalinosporamide
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. salL disruption blocked native chlorinated precursor formation. 2. Feeding with 5'-fluoro-5'-deoxyadenosine redirected biosynthesis to fluorosalinosporamide. 3. This produced an analog with altered proteasome inhibition behavior.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classic mutasynthesis example in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces fluorine into precursor metabolism to support fluorinated analog production
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Insertion of flA bypassed the need for exogenous 5'-FDA feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous gene insertion with analog production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. flA from S. cattleya was introduced into the salinosporamide producer. 2. This bypassed precursor feeding for fluorosalinosporamide production. 3. The work demonstrates pathway augmentation for halogenated analog biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review describes this as a follow-up engineering improvement.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Central alkaloid-pathway enzyme condensing tryptamine-derived precursor with secologanin to form strictosidine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Binding-pocket mutations expanded substrate specificity and enabled production of unnatural alkaloids with precursor feeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein engineering, plant transformation, precursor feeding, metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Strictosidine synthase was engineered by selective active-site mutation. 2. The engineered enzyme accepted altered substrates for mutasynthesis. 3. Transformed C. roseus cells produced diverse unnatural alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a prominent example of enzyme engineering for diversification.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces conjugated double bonds into phytoene during carotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directed evolution generated a variant producing tetradehydrolycopene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Directed evolution with visual pigment screen
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Directed evolution of phytoene desaturase altered product outcome in a carotenoid pathway. 2. A pink variant producing tetradehydrolycopene was isolated. 3. This demonstrates visual screening for pathway-enzyme engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source host not explicitly stated in excerpt.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes linear carotenoids to form cyclic carotenoid products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Gene shuffling produced mutant cyclase variants including one generating torulene via a novel pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lycopene cyclase variants were generated by gene shuffling. 2. A mutant producing torulene via a novel route was isolated. 3. The study exemplifies enzyme engineering for new carotenoid products.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as example of diversification by directed evolution.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes farnesyl diphosphate into sesquiterpene products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Active-site engineering altered product specificity to seven alternative terpenes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. γ-Humulene synthase is naturally promiscuous. 2. Saturation mutagenesis identified residues controlling product plasticity. 3. Variants specifically produced seven alternative cyclization products.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper discusses it as a proof-of-principle enzyme-engineering example.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Pathway-specific transcription factor, Streptomyces antibiotic regulatory protein
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression caused nearly sixfold increase in antibiotic production
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive pathway-specific activator enhancing cluster output
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A SARP regulator in the fredericamycin cluster positively controlled antibiotic production. 2. Overexpression gave nearly sixfold higher production. 3. The review highlights SARPs as common engineering targets in Streptomyces clusters.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact regulator gene name not specified in excerpt.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Pathway-specific transcription factor, Streptomyces antibiotic regulatory protein
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased mithramycin titer 16-fold and could activate actinorhodin pathway in S. coelicolor
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong positive activator of antibiotic production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in native and heterologous contexts
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtmR overexpression strongly enhanced mithramycin production. 2. It also activated the actinorhodin pathway when expressed in S. coelicolor. 3. The review presents it as an especially powerful cluster activator.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Illustrates cross-cluster activation potential of some SARPs.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased citrinin production about 400-fold in a heterologous host carrying the biosynthetic cluster
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Activation of transferred biosynthetic gene cluster
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Native activator required for strong expression after pathway transfer
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ctnA is a key native activator of the citrinin cluster. 2. In a heterologous A. oryzae host, its overexpression improved production by 400-fold over the single-copy baseline. 3. The example underscores the importance of transferring pathway regulators with gene clusters.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also relevant to activation of cryptic fungal pathways.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Gene inactivation improved platensimycin and platencin titers by 100-fold over wild type
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Negative regulation of biosynthetic gene cluster
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major pathway-specific repressor constraining antibiotic output
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The platensimycin/platencin cluster contains a GntR-family repressor. 2. Inactivation derepressed production and increased titers 100-fold. 3. The study demonstrates the value of removing negative regulators to enhance pathway flux.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact gene symbol not given in excerpt.
